title: cycnium dentiflorum
intro: Cycnium dentiflorum, also known as "bitter apple" or "fruit of the vine", is a plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally used in medicine, the plant's roots and leaves have been reported to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Cycnium dentiflorum include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant's roots and leaves are often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
